narrative.headline: 2022 Jeep Wrangler: electrical issues lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 358 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is by far the most reported area at 41%, followed by the engine (23%) and steering (20%). No deaths are reported. The electrical complaints also include the most fires of any area (9), so that system is worth a close look before you buy.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 147 reports (41%), and the most fires of any area at 9, plus 2 crashes and 2 injuries. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.sample: The Wrangler accelerated while doing regenerative braking or while my foot was on the brake. This caused it to hit a building. It is available for inspection. My safety and my child's safety was put at risk when contact was made with a building. Those inside the building were als
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 83 reports (23%), tied to 3 crashes, 2 fires, and 1 injury. Watch for power loss or warning lights while driving.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 72 reports (20%), with 1 crash, 1 fire, and 1 injury. Feel for looseness or wandering at highway speed on a test drive.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 55 reports (15%), linked to 2 crashes and no fires or injuries. Some reports describe stalling or warning lights while driving.

issues.title: Suspension
issues.blurb: 53 reports (15%), with 1 crash and no fires or injuries. A smaller but recurring complaint area.
